arts.ttl: Hull City transfer exit confirmed as versatile forward reacts after sealing League One switch
arts.sum: Longman watched his new club in action last night as Wrexham secured a hard-fought point at home to promotion rivals Birmingham City last night. He said in a statement on Wrexham's club website: “I’m over the moon and so glad that the deal is over the line.

arts.ttl: Hull City predicted starting XI for Sheffield United as big change made following transfer blow
arts.sum: City's predicted starting XI: Pandur, Jacob, Burns, Jones, Drameh, Alzate, Slater, Puerta, Gelhardt, Kamara, Pedro. Selles also faces a dilemma at the back with Matty Jacob pushing for a return to the starting line-up, having seen Sean McLoughlin play at left-back in the last two games, with Fin Burns operating in the middle alongside Alfie Jones and Cody Drameh playing on the right in the absence of skipper Coyle.

arts.ttl: How Hull City's Ruben Selles is using Brentford boss Thomas Frank to aid Tigers frustration
arts.sum: City will again be backed by a big away following in South Yorkshire, and Selles says there will be times in the game where the fans can play an important role, just as the 1,700-strong Tigers contingent did at The Den last Saturday. Tuesday night's 2-1 home defeat to QPR left City inherently frustrated with their inability again to back up a draw with Leeds United and the 1-0 win at Millwall last Saturday, and with Portsmouth beating Stoke City on Wednesday evening, the Tigers were dumped back in the bottom three ahead of Friday night's trip to promotion-chasing Sheffield United.

arts.ttl: AS Monaco and Ipswich Town hovering to sign Sheffield United player
arts.sum: AS Monaco and Ipswich Town are among the clubs in the picture to sign Sheffield United’s Ryan Oné. That’s according to L’Equipe, who state the Ligue 1 side are monitoring the 18-year-old’s situation. Ipswich Town, for their part, also appreciate his profile.
